source_comments: Your Lie In April
Greed the Avaricious in Fullmetal Alchemist Brotherhood.
The real question is why would they be your GF? Doesn't matter who you can choose if she would just dump you immediately..

Hinomaru Sumo - National Tressures

Seven Deadly Sins - Seven Deadly Sins and Ten commandments

Shokugeki no Soma - Elite Ten Council 

Katanagatari - Deviant Blades

Demon Slayer - 12 kizuki and 9 hashira
Nodame Cantabile and Haikyuu. I really enjoy other shows, but I find comfort in anime based on real world.

I like both, but HxH has better progress in terms of character and story development in my opinion. YYH has good fights and interesting characters, but there is not that much of a development and the story was a little repetitive for me. Stronger opponent, training, win and repeat.
I would say Power rangers, but not sure about the number of people or about being it an anime.

I have been watching One piece for more then 15 years now and rewatched it twice since, but I have to say, if you are not hooked, don't force it. There is no thing as best anime for everyone I think. Also I have to admit that nowadays I am watching it just out of habit. If you watch Water 7 arc and don't get hooked, I think it is better not to spend more time on it. For me personally it is the greatest arc so far.

I definitely agree. The other thing about Haikyuu or Dr. Stone I like are the perspectives of both sides. The other side is not bad just because they are enemies, but they have reasons for what they are doing. Not being evil just for the sake of them being opponents of our main protagonists.
You're welcome. Hope you enjoy it.

The show has great characters and is not only about the games. I like when different characters look, act and think differently and this anime delivers that. I can also recommend Run with the wind. Sport anime, similar feel, same music artist I think, who makes spot on OST.
I think Todo from Jujutsu Kaisen and Magneto from X-men. You know.. just..Jennifer Lawrence thing..
Sword Art Online
Akame Ga Kill!
Hikaru no go
I would say Hajime no Ippo? I think it is based on real boxers and boxing techniques. Obviously it is still overexaggerated a little.
If you like classical music I would recommend Piano no Mori or Nodame Cantabile, which are about music and have great music.

HunterXHunter or Fullmetal Alchemist Brotherhood are in my opinion great for getting into anime. Or JoJo for references and memes
Just grow up.
Fallie-slowie —parachute
